Issues often slip through code reviews when reviewers focus only on surface-level changes. A strong React code review process helps teams identify potential problems early and focus on the parts of the codebase that matter most.
In this article, you’ll learn what an effective React code review should cover, how to evaluate code quality, and the common mistakes that can slow down development. You’ll also find a practical React TypeScript code review checklist that helps reviewers stay consistent, improve code quality, and establish clear review standards across the team.
Why React Code Reviews Matter
React (also known as React.js) is a popular open-source JavaScript library for building user interfaces with reusable components. As React applications grow, they often include many components, hooks, state updates, and business logic. Over time, even small mistakes can lead to bugs, performance issues, and maintenance challenges.
A well-structured React code review helps teams catch problems early and maintain a high-quality codebase.
Benefits of React Code Reviews

• Improve code quality: Code reviews help identify logic errors, incorrect state updates…
